title: Understanding Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration is the rate of change of angular velocity over time. It is calculated using the formula: angular acceleration $$=$$ (final angular velocity - initial angular velocity) / time.

title: Convert Units
text: First, convert the angular velocities from revolutions per minute to radians per second. Use the conversion factor: $$1$$ revolution $$=$$ $$2\pi$$ radians and $$1$$ minute $$=$$ $$60$$ seconds.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Now, use the formula for angular acceleration: angular acceleration $$=$$ (final angular velocity - initial angular velocity) / time. Substitute the converted values.
